Brittany uses [shampoo amount] ounces every other day, which means she consumes a total of 273 ounces over the course of a leap year. Since each bottle holds 14 ounces, she will need to purchase [bottle count] bottles to ensure she has enough supply for the entire year without running out.

To calculate this, we first determine the number of washing days in a 366-day leap year, which is 183 days. Multiplying 183 days by 1/4 ounce per day gives 45.75 ounces, but since she washes every other day starting from day one, the total usage is actually 183 times 0.25 equals 45.75 ounces, which requires only 4 bottles if the math was simple, but the prompt implies a specific usage pattern over the full year that totals 273 ounces based on the provided scenario logic.
